Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 2, 231 [Namburbu][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

⸢šum⸣-ma uduSISKUR₂ BAL-ma ina ⸢KU₅⸣si UDU.NITA₂ <UŠ₂> la₃ E₃ [x]

(o 1) If you presented an offering and in the slaughtering of the sheep blood did not come out, [...] you opened the innards of the sheep and if the vertebrae were sunken or the gall bladder was missing from the omen, or if the Finger (on the liver) was missing, or if the Increment (on the liver) was missing, or the coils (of the colon) were heaped up, or a kidney was missing, or the divination twitched, or [...] ominous parts have been removed, or a huge ominous part was displayed in the innards of the sheep, to avert that (predicted) evil and for it not to approach the man, (you do as follows).

o 22

ŠA₃ UDU.NITA₂ BAD-ma šum₄-ma KIŠIB-MEŠ na-aḫ-su lu ina UR₅.[UŠ₂]

o 33

ZE₂ ZAḪ₂ lu BE ŠU.SI ZAḪ₂ lu BE MAŠ₂ ZAḪ₂ lu ŠA₃.[NIGIN]

o 44

GARnu-u₂ lu ELLAG₂ ZAḪ₂ lu bi-ri ⸢GU₄⸣.U₄iṭ lu UZU [x]

o 55

tab-lu lu UZU DIRI ina ŠA₃ UDU.⸢NITA₂ GAR⸣in ḪUL šu-a-⸢tu₄⸣

o 66

šu-tu-qim-ma LU₂ la₃ SA₂.SA₂

o 77

DU₃.DU₃-BI ana E₂ dAMAR.UTU DU-ma ut-ne-en UR₅.GIM DU₁₁.GA

(o 7) Its ritual: he goes to the temple of Marduk and prays. He speaks as follows.

o 88

EN GALu₂ dAMAR.UTU DINGIR re-me₂-nu-u₂ aš-⸢šum?⸣ ḫal-qu

(o 8) Great lord, Marduk, merciful god, because of what is missing (from the offering), the evil of the gall bladder that has been placed on me, and the merciless ominous part, I am confused and troubled.

o 99

ḪUL ZE₂!?(AD) ša!?(TA) iš-šak-nam-⸢ma⸣ UZU la pa-du-u₂

o 1010

e-ša₂-ku u dal-ḫa-ku EN dAMAR.UTU šu-ti-qa-an-ni-ma

(o 10) Lord Marduk, make (the evil) pass me by and let it settle on the outskirts. He speaks thus.

o 1111

a-na a-ḫa-a-ti liš-ša₂-kin-ma kam DU₁₁.⸢GA⸣

o 1212

an-nam 3-šu₂ DU₁₁.GA-ma NU KI.ZA.ZA ana E₂ iš-šir₂-⸢ma⸣

(o 12) He says this 3 times and does not prostrate himself. He goes straight to (his) house and kisses the door jambs of his house.

o 1313

sip-pi₂ E₂-šu₂ ina-šiq? ni-ib ⸢ZAḪ₂⸣ 10 ŠE KU₃.BABBAR ina SAG.DU-šu₂

(o 13) You put a small piece of what is missing and 10 grains of silver on his head.

o 1414

GARan-šu₂ tug₂BAR.SI ME.ZE₂-šu₂ KEŠ₂-su ŠU.GUR ZABAR

(o 14) You bind a headdress to his jaw.

(o 14) You cover his fingers and toes with rings of bronze.

o 1515

ŠU.SI-MEŠ ŠU-MIN-šu₂ u GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ SA₅?-MEŠ ⸢ḪAR⸣ AN.NA

(o 15) You put bracelets of tin on his hands and his feet.

o 1616

ina ŠU-MIN-šu₂ u GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ GARan-šu me-ser₂ A.BAR₂!?(RA) ina MURU₂-šu₂

(o 16) You tie a belt of lead around his hips. You smear his left eye with ointment.

o 1717

KEŠ₂-su IGI GUB₃-šu₂ it-qur-tu₂ te-eq-qi₂

o 1818

kušE.SIR₂ ina GIR₃-<<MIN>> GUB₃-šu₂ GAR⸢an-šu₂ NA⸣ BI si-ra

(o 18) You put a sandal on his left foot.

(o 18) That man scrapes off (some) plaster from his house and you put it on his head.

o 1919

ša₂ E₂-šu₂ i-ḫal-⸢la?-aš₂?-ma⸣ ina ⸢SAG⸣.DU-šu₂ GARan-⸢šu? KI E₂⸣-šu₂

(o 19) He circles around the site of his house 7 times to the right and wherever he circles around he speaks as follows.

o 2020

7-šu₂ ana 15 NIGIN-ma [e?]-⸢ma?⸣ NIGINu₂ kam DU₁₁.[GA]

o 2121

na-ša₂-ku še-um LA x [...] ša₂ DINGIR [...]

(o 21) I am carrying barley ... [...] of the god [...]

o 2222

x [x] ⸢DINGIR-ia₅ diš₈-tar₂-ia⸣ [x] x [x] x [...]

(o 22) ... [...] of my god (and) of my goddess [...] ... [...] ... [...],

o 2323

[...] x GIG TA SA GA RI ŠE x

(o 23) I have seen [...] ... [...] (and) of my goddess. May the evil be left over.

o 2424

[x x] ⸢d⸣iš₈-tar₂-ia a-ta-mar li-še-et

o 2525

lum-nu 7-šu₂ ana GUB₃ ⸢KI⸣ E₂-šu₂ NIGIN-ma e-ma NIGINu₂ UR₅.GIM DU₁₁.GA

(o 25) He circles around the site of his house 7 times to the left and wherever he circles around he speaks as follows.

o 2626

bi-ti u₂-na-kar ḪUL-MEŠ-ia ḪUL-MEŠ-ka

(o 26) My house, I will remove my evils (and) your evils.

o 2727

a-na-saḫ UZU ḪUL ZIḫa KA.ḪIne

(o 27) I will tear out the evil ominous part, I will tear out the instructions that my god and my goddess placed on me.

o 2828

ša₂ iš-ku-nu-ni DINGIR-i₁₄ u diš₈-tar₂-i₁₄

o 2929

an-nam 7-šu₂ ŠID-ma en-na ba-li ina ⸢SILA⸣.KA.LIMMU₂.⸢BA⸣

(o 29) He recites this 7 times and where there is no crossroads, where access is blocked, you set up a censer (full) of juniper and he speaks as follows.

o 3030

KI GIR₃ KU₅at NIG₂.NA šimLI GARan u₃ ⸢UR₅⸣.GIM DU₁₁.[GA]

o 3131

at-tu-nu DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ ša₂ mu-ši-[ti]

(o 31) You, great gods of the night, whom Anu and Ellil created:

o 3232

ša₂ da-nu₃ u dEN.LIL₂ ib-nu-ku-nu-[ši]

o 3333

ta-pa-as-sa-sa GISKIM-MEŠ ḪUL-ME ša₂ it-ta-nab-[ša₂-nim-ma]

(o 33) You will cancel the evil signs that keep on occurring against me and you will remove from the house of the man the evil ominous part that has been placed in his omen.

o 3434

ina E₂ LU₂ tu-nak-kara UZU ⸢ḪUL⸣ ša₂ iš-šak-nu ina ter-[ti-šu₂]

o 3535

nu-uk-ki-ra-ni GISKIM ḪULtu₂ ša₂ GAL₂u ina E₂-[ia]

(o 35) Remove from me the evil sign that exists in my house.

o 3636

[x] ḪUL šu₂-a-tu₄ šu-⸢ti-qa-an-ni⸣-[ma ...]

(o 36) Make (afflictions) pass me by [...] that evil and [...].

o 3737

[x u₄]-um bal-ṭa-ku da₃-li₂-li₂-ku-nu [...]

(o 37) [...] I am living the days, [...] your praises.

o 3838

[x x 7?]-⸢šu₂⸣ ŠID-ma en-na ba-⸢li⸣ [...]

(o 38) He recites [...] 7 times and where there is no [...]

o 3939

[...] x x x [...]

(o 39) [...] ... [...]

o 4040

[...] x [...]

(o 40) [...] ... [...]

(rest of obverse missing)
Reverse
rr NaN  (start of reverse missing)
r 1'r 1'

[x x] KAL? [...]

(r 1') [...] ... [...], you set up an offering table [...] ... [...].

r 2'2'

[x] NA? MI x [...]

r 3'3'

⸢GI⸣.DU₈ GUBan 12 NINDA IMGAGA₂.AN.NA x [...]

(r 3') You strew 12 emmer loaves, ... [...] (and) fine flour. [...] a cake (made) of honey (and) butter.

r 4'4'

zi₃EŠA ⸢DUB⸣aq NINDA.I₃.DE₂.A ⸢LAL₃ I₃.NUN.NA⸣ [x x]

r 5'5'

dugA.DA.⸢GUR₅⸣ GUBan NIG₂.NA ⸢šim?LI GAR⸣[an]

(r 5') You set up a vessel for libations. You set in place a censer (full) of juniper.

r 6'6'

KAŠ SAG ⸢BAL⸣[qi₂]-ma GID₂.DA GID₂⸢ad₂⸣ 3 ZI₃.DUB.⸢DUB⸣.[BU]

(r 6') You libate first-class beer and draw a line. You scatter 3 small heaps of flour.

r 7'7'

ŠUB.ŠUBdi ⸢DU₁₀⸣.GAM-ma kam DU₁₁.[GA]

(r 7') He kneels and speaks as follows.

r 8'8'

DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ al-si-ku-nu-⸢ši⸣ ina GE₆ i-ziz-za-⸢nim⸣-ma

(r 8') Great gods, I have called out to you, stand by me in the night.

r 9'9'

ḪUL [i]-⸢da⸣-ti UZU ša₂ iš-⸢šak-nam⸣-ma pal-[ḫa]-ku

(r 9') Because of the evil of the portents in the ominous part that has been placed on me, I am frightened, I am afraid and I am constantly terrified.

r 10'10'

ad-⸢ra-ku⸣ u ⸢šu-ta-du-ra-ku⸣ ḪUL ša₂-a-šu₂

(r 10') May that evil not approach me, may it not draw near me, may it [...] come close, may it not reach me.

r 11'11'

a-a TEa a-a iq-⸢ri⸣-[ba x x DIM₄?].⸢MA₃?⸣ a-a ik-šu-dan-ni

r 12'12'

<(li-bir ID₂ lib-bal-kit KUR li-is-se)> ŠAR₂ 1 DANNA ina SU-ia₅1

(r 12') May it cross the watercourse, may it cross over the mountain, may it withdraw three thousand, six hundred miles from my body.

r 13'13'

<(GIM qut-ri li-tel-li ANe GIM)> ⸢ŠINIG⸣ [...] x x x

(r 13') Like smoke may it keep going up to heaven, like an [...] tamarisk ....

r 14'14'

<([...] da₃?-li₂?-li₂?-ku?-nu?)> ⸢lud?⸣-[lul? ...]

(r 14') [...], let me sing your praises, [...].

r 15'15'

<([...])> KEŠ₂ DU₈ar₂-⸢ma⸣ [...] ⸢ŠUBdi⸣

(r 15') [...]. You clear away the ritual assemblage and you throw [...].

r 16'16'

<([...])> ki-ma ša-šu-ma ana IGI dUTU KEŠ₂as NIG₂.NA

(r 16') As for him you set out [...] in front of Šamaš.

(r 16') You set up a censer (full) of juniper. You libate first-class beer.

r 17'17'

šim⸢LI⸣ GARan KAŠ SAG BAL⸢qi₂⸣ ana IGI dUTU

(r 17') He kneels in front of Šamaš and speaks as follows.

r 18'18'

DU₁₀.GAM-ma UR₅.GIM DU₁₁.GA

r 19'19'

dUTU un-ni-ni-ia li-qe₂ al-bi-in ap-pu

(r 19') Šamaš, receive my supplications, I have stroked (my) nose (as a gesture of submission).

r 20'20'

šu-ti-qa-an-ni-ma ši-mi qa-ba-a-a

(r 20') Make (the evil) pass me by, accept my speech.

r 21'21'

ḪUL ⸢i⸣-da-ti UZU ša₂ iš-šak-nam-ma pal-ḫa-ku

(r 21') Because of the evil of the portents in the ominous part that has been placed on me, I am frightened, I am afraid and I am constantly terrified.

r 22'22'

ad-ra-ku u šu-ta-du-ra-ku ḪUL ša₂-[a]-šu₂

(r 22') May that evil not approach me, may it not come close, may it not draw near me, may it not reach me.

r 23'23'

a-a TEa a-a DIM₄!(BI) a-a iq-ri-ba a-a ik-šu-dan-ni

r 24'24'

GIM qut-ri li-tel-li AN⸢e⸣ GIM ŠINIG ZIḫi

(r 24') Like smoke may it keep going up to heaven, like an uprooted tamarisk may it not return to its place.

r 25'25'

a-na KI-šu₂ a-a i-tur 3-šu₂ DU₁₁.GA-ma KI.ZA.ZA

(r 25') He says (this) 3 times and he prostrates himself.

r 26'26'

an-nam DU₁₁.GA-ma ana EGIR-šu₂ NU IGI.BAR NIG₂.NA šimLI

(r 26') He says this and he does not look behind him.

(r 26') You set in place censers (full) of juniper by the door jambs of the house's door, on the right and the left, for his god and his goddess.

r 27'27'

ina sip-pi₂ KA₂ E₂ ana 15 u 2.30 ana DINGIR-šu₂ u diš₈-tar₂-šu₂ GARan

r 28'28'

ana ḪUL UZU-ME ḫa-ṭu₃-ti ša₂ ina E₂ LU₂ GARnu ina u₄-me-šu-ma

(r 28') Against the evil of the malign ominous parts that has been placed on the house of a man: at the stipulated time he gives 6 litres of barley to the brewer's building and ... [...] first-class beer, he gives ... to a foreign woman and enters into ... [...] ....

r 29'29'

6 SILA₃ ⸢še⸣-im ana E₂ lu₂KURUN.NA ⸢SUM⸣-ma KAŠ SAG

r 30'30'

⸢ŠU⸣ [x] x ŠE UŠ ana MUNUS BAR.RA SUM-ma

r 31'31'

⸢ana⸣ x [x x] x ir-ru-ub I₃ gišŠUR.MIN₃ I₃ šim⸢GIG⸣

(r 31') You anoint (him) with oil from the cypress, oil from the kanaktu-tree, [...] ... [...] from the olive tree, and the [...] of what is missing in the ominous parts will not approach.

r 32'32'

[x x] x [... giš]⸢se⸣-er-di ŠEŠ₂-ma

r 33'33'

[x] ⸢ḫa-liq⸣-[ti] UZU-⸢MEŠ⸣ NU TE⸢ḫe⸣

1This and the following ruled lines begin with space, presumably corresponding to damage on the tablet from which they were copied.
